title: Behavioural scenario verdict
description: Structured judge output for one behavioural scenario run by the skill-tester. Emitted per scenario so results are diffable and CI-gradeable.

invocation_type.description: explicit=names the skill/artifact; implicit=describes the need only; contextual=implicit + noisy context; negative=should NOT fire this skill

expected_skill.description: Skill that should fire. null for negative controls (no skill should fire, or a different one).

selected_skill.description: Skill the subagent actually routed to (read its SKILL.md / acted on it). null if none fired.

routing.description: pass if selected_skill matches expectation (for negatives: pass if the skill under test did NOT fire)

usage.description: Whether the skill was used within its constraints. n/a for negative controls.

checks.description: One entry per PASS/anti-pattern assertion from the catalog scenario.

tool_calls.description: Ordered operations the subagent actually performed (self-reported in v1; real tool events in headless/CI). Used to grade process goals.

flaky.description: true if a re-run changed the result

evidence.description: Short quote from the transcript / tool trace supporting the verdict

response_text.description: The model's final response text, recorded for the report and --debug output
